text: body. It was as though the jaguar colluded to distract the twins, who were guarding the serpent, which came out of the pyramid-temple to swallow me whole. After the serpent swallowed me, my attention was relocated to the underworld and the mid-heaven. Theoretically, the mammalian brain held the attention of the frontal lobes, allowing the (serpentine) sensory nervous system to operate (without the constraints of the frontal lobes) as a ladder, so the perceiver (in the spirit and the soul) could visit the upper and lower worlds. The circumstances of Sanna’s life and death, and the Lifting the Shadow ceremony, awakened the heart mind, revealing a clairvoyance of an otherworld and the afterlife. The impression that autonomous “great forces in the spirit” dwell on the other side of a veil and may never appear to the waking mind’s eye, gives me pause and I wonder: What else am I not seeing? What other mythical, legendary, or unknown wonders have simply chosen not to step out from behind the veil of ignorance? A good grasp of archetypal psychology, alchemy, and metaphysics provides a foundation for knowing the hypnogogic experience intelligently, for reducing things psychologically when necessary, and for seeing things as transpersonal and therefore with less personal morbidity. So much of the world is upset and transforming quickly. The rational mind may not find all the answers. To integrate the extraordinary gifts of the soul, we can practice meditating, concentrating on awakening the heart mind, focusing attention inward for the shift. This may help humanity find many practical solutions that are simply not available to the externalized, rational mind. I believe this vision in Tulum can add to our understanding of the human condition and ancient, esoteric philosophy and iconography, which empirical science can neither prove nor disprove. All the best spiritual traditions encourage us to seek the truth, to meditate, to open our hearts, and to have patience. In the current climate of anxiety, climate change, civil unrest, and imbalances beyond measure, we can achieve balance by cultivating the heart, thereby developing an awareness of the liminal realm and cultivating conditions for a hypnogogic vision, wherein sacred forces in the spirit may appear to awaken the heart. Sanna’s many gifts in life were overshadowed only by this journey to the afterlife, through a cosmic portal, and an encounter with a serpent ladder at Samna, near the ancient ruins of Tulum. 22 INTO THE GREEN UNDERWORLD DAENIN TEJEDA PLANT WISDOM For most of my adulthood, I have been irritated with ungrounded yet hugely gifted creative people who have shown me the value of healthy skepticism. I have always been empathic myself, and my ability to “see things” has been developed and tempered with testing and observing repeatable results, using methods requiring considerable practice through spiritual exercises. I had a good reason to develop my senses, as my work as a healer required it to be effective. I began to notice other things affecting clients or friends as my work progressed. Although I had never smoked marijuana myself, I noticed that the partaker of this plant became susceptible to the influences of foreign entities. I saw these entities in people’s auras as smoky wisps and was greatly disturbed by their presence. Once a person began to smoke, I would see in plain sight a smoky, wispy mirage on his or her shoulders. Empathically it had a presence; I could feel something there. Out of love, great respect, and curiosity—and concern for my friends—I decided to investigate further to understand the true nature of these beings, so I could be in a position to inform my friends and myself. If they were good influences, I would find out. Maybe they were just foreign to me, and I didn’t understand them. If they were detrimental, I could protect my friends by at least giving them information to make a more informed choice about their use of marijuana. Since I had never felt drawn to marijuana, this felt like a sacrifice. But I asked myself, “Who else, if not me, could do this or could give me a satisfactory answer?” Ultimately, it was this decision to help my friends and find the truth of the nature of these smoky wisp spirits that led me into the green underworld. Prior to that very intentional journey one night in May of 2005, what I saw in users was this: an expression of wide-lipped satisfaction; a vacant, emotionally numb demeanor; and a sense of lowered extrasensory perception and mental clarity. Often (almost always, actually—99.9 percent of the time) I saw signs of the weakening of the will of those who used it. And in about 85 percent of overall cases, I saw the worst side of its use (which has more to do with the user than the plant or its spirit, in my opinion), and this was the sickening, fiendish dependency on it to maintain balance. The users could not stay somewhere if there was not a fresh supply of it. They would not leave home for fear that they would run out of it and become “stranded” without their crutch. They would begin speaking urgently and with a rationalizing hypocrisy always in defense of their “fun” addiction. But this was not really just a physical addiction in the works. It was far more complex. My concern for my friends’ well-being and distress at the unknown nature of these wispy visions had focused my will and preparatory psychic protection ritual to a razor-sharpness; I had a lot of emotional intensity behind my will to see the truth about these plant spirits. We all sat in a circle, and the last words I heard before my first taste of this medicine was, “Be really present.” Then with a nod and a gesture my journey began. During the experience of being high, which accelerated from around 9:00 p.m. to a 3:00 a.m. peak, I was aware of very little. I noticed that most of my psychic abilities were diminished
